perturbation.original_text: Party B shall pay performance deposit to Party A on time and in full amount as agreed herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B shall pay a performance deposit to Party A. The amount and timing of this deposit are subject to further negotiation and may not be required.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that Party B 'shall pay performance deposit to Party A on time and in full amount', establishing a clear obligation. By contrast, the modified text states that 'the amount and timing of this deposit are subject to further negotiation and may not be required.' This introduces uncertainty, contradicting the initial mandatory deposit by making it conditional and potentially waivable. This ambiguity could result in disputes over whether Party B is actually obligated to pay a performance deposit. The change from a mandatory requirement to a negotiable item creates a direct conflict within the text, where previously the 'original_text' garanteed the enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party B shall pay a performance deposit to Party A. The amount and timing of this deposit are subject to further negotiation and may not be required.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text imposed a clear obligation on Party B to pay a performance deposit. The modified text introduces uncertainty by stating that the amount and timing are subject to negotiation and the deposit 'may not be required,' therefore, the changed text contradicts the original agreement. Also, the changed and contradicted texts are exactly the same which creates a contradiction within the contract itself. The shift from a mandatory deposit to a conditional one creates an inconsistency that affects the enforceability of the deposit clause.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall settle the payment every month, and the initial settlement period shall be the end of the third month, which means that the information service fee generated in the first month shall be paid to the account of the partner by the end of the third month.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall make an effort to settle the payment, although the exact schedule may depend on factors beyond our control.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that 'The Parties shall settle the payment every month, and the initial settlement period shall be the end of the third month, which means that the information service fee generated in the first month shall be paid to the account of the partner by the end of the third month.' By contrast, the modified text states that 'The Parties shall make an effort to settle the payment, although the exact schedule may depend on factors beyond our control.'. This introduces uncertainty, contradicting the original mandatory deposit by making it conditional and uncertain, potentially making the settlement waivable. This ambiguity could result in disputes over whether Party B is actually obligated to pay a performance deposit, creating an In-Text Contradiction. It is also making the terms uneforcable
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall make an effort to settle the payment, although the exact schedule may depend on factors beyond our control.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a specific payment schedule, creating a clear obligation. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating that the payment schedule depends on factors beyond control, contradicting the original mandatory nature of the payment and potentially making it unenforceable. The contradiction is strong because it fundamentally alters the commitment to a fixed payment schedule, undermining the clarity and enforceability of the payment terms.
